explanation: TRUE POSITIVE. The acceptAdmin → adminGrantStep2 → addAccount → ARI.add path definitively bypasses RoleLenMax. During step-1 (proposal execution via roleApplyRequestsFromStore), addAccount only sets pending[account].role = Admin and returns early without incrementing admins.length, so _roleRangeCheck() passes with the unchanged length (e.g., 5 == RoleLenMax). During step-2 (acceptAdmin call), adminGrantStep2 sets ai.nonce = NonceAtInit and calls addAccount again, which takes the step-2 branch: deletes pending entry and calls ARI.add() unconditionally, pushing to aris.admins and exceeding RoleLenMax with no validation. _roleRangeCheck() is a private function called only from roleApplyRequestsFromCd and roleApplyRequestsFromStore — never from adminGrantStep2 — and ARI.add() contains no array-length bounds check. Any subsequent role proposal execution will call _roleRangeCheck(), which checks admins.length <= RoleLenMax and reverts, blocking all future governance actions until an admin-removal proposal successfully recovers the count. The root cause, call chain, missing check, and impact are all confirmed by direct code inspection.
